verses.text: 'Truly my soul waiteth upon God,' for 'he only is my rock and my salvation,' so David shall not be greatly moved. He rests on God alone. The soul that waits quietly on God finds a stability nothing can topple.

'Truly my soul waiteth upon God,' for 'he only is my rock and my salvation,' so David shall not be greatly moved. He rests on God alone. The soul that waits quietly on God finds a stability nothing can topple.
He asks his foes how long they will assail him, who bless with the mouth but curse inwardly. Their friendliness is false. Flattery that hides hostility is among the wicked's favourite weapons.
He asks his foes how long they will assail him, who bless with the mouth but curse inwardly. Their friendliness is false. Flattery that hides hostility is among the wicked's favourite weapons.

He charges his own soul again, 'wait thou only upon God; for my expectation is from him,' his rock and refuge. He repeats the resolve to himself. Waiting on God is a discipline the heart must renew against every doubt.

He charges his own soul again, 'wait thou only upon God; for my expectation is from him,' his rock and refuge. He repeats the resolve to himself. Waiting on God is a discipline the heart must renew against every doubt.

He charges his own soul again, 'wait thou only upon God; for my expectation is from him,' his rock and refuge. He repeats the resolve to himself. Waiting on God is a discipline the heart must renew against every doubt.
'Trust in him at all times... pour out your heart before him: God is a refuge for us.' He turns to urge others. The God who is our refuge invites us to empty our whole heart before Him.

Men high and low are mere vanity, lighter than breath; trust not in oppression, robbery, or rising riches. Earthly props are empty. To set the heart on wealth is to lean on what cannot bear our weight.

Men high and low are mere vanity, lighter than breath; trust not in oppression, robbery, or rising riches. Earthly props are empty. To set the heart on wealth is to lean on what cannot bear our weight.

God has spoken plainly that 'power belongeth unto God' and to Him belongs mercy, for He repays each according to his work. Strength and mercy meet in Him. The Almighty is also the merciful, and He judges all in righteousness.

God has spoken plainly that 'power belongeth unto God' and to Him belongs mercy, for He repays each according to his work. Strength and mercy meet in Him. The Almighty is also the merciful, and He judges all in righteousness.
